title: Force unwrap on client.user! in chat privilege checks can crash server
description: Multiple chat handler methods force-unwrap client.user! without checking for nil first: createPublicChat (line 121), deletePublicChat (line 156), createPrivateChat (line 198), and setTopic (line 432). Although receiveMessage in ServerController checks client.state for CONNECTED and GAVE_CLIENT_INFO states, the state machine only blocks specific messages. A client in LOGGED_IN state where client.user is nil (e.g., due to a race or unexpected state) would cause a fatal crash on force-unwrap, taking down the server or at minimum the client handler thread. The pattern 'guard let user = client.user else { replyError... }' is correctly used in receiveMessageSendMessage (line 872) but not in the chat handlers.
recommendation: Replace all client.user! force-unwraps with guard let user = client.user else { replyError(...); return } pattern, consistent with the message handlers.
